1. Hierarchical Architecture

4-tier memory + KL-constrained router

ArchitectureMATHIR core
Figure 1 — 1. Hierarchical Architecture

MATHIR is a 4-tier memory system sandwiched between any modality encoder (text, image, audio, video) and any LLM. Working memory holds the last N steps; episodic memory stores past experiences; semantic memory clusters concepts; and immunological memory detects anomalies. A KL-constrained router fuses them without collapsing into a single mode.

2. The 4 Memory Tiers

Working · Episodic · Semantic · Immunological

MemoryV7.8.0 stable
Figure 2 — 2. The 4 Memory Tiers

Each tier has its own capacity, update rate, and retrieval algorithm. Working memory (64 slots, O(1) ring buffer) feeds short-term attention. Episodic memory (1000 slots, cosine k-NN) stores unique events. Semantic memory (256 prototypes, online k-means) compresses recurring patterns. Immunological memory (100 patterns, Mahalanobis distance) flags out-of-distribution inputs — proven Neyman-Pearson optimal (Theorem 4).

3. Retrieval Quality Comparison

5 systems · 50 domain queries · 200 chunks (White, Fluid Mechanics)

V7.1 researchD wins
Figure 3 — 3. Retrieval Quality Comparison

We benchmarked 5 retrieval strategies on a real textbook corpus. The default V7 retrieval (64-dim projection) underperforms at 19.7%. Approach D — a hybrid of BM25 sparse recall, dense embeddings, and a cross-encoder reranker — wins at 45.7%, a +14.1 percentage point improvement over FAISS.

\mathrm{quality}(D) = \frac{|\mathrm{top}_5(D) \cap \mathrm{GT}|}{5} = 45.7\%

4. Speed–Quality Pareto Frontier

Lower-left = fast & poor · Upper-right = slow & rich

ParetoA / D / FAISS
Figure 4 — 4. Speed–Quality Pareto Frontier

There is no free lunch. FAISS raw vectors are 0.05 ms but cap at 31.6% quality. Approach D reaches 45.7% but takes 494 ms. Approach A (raw embedding bypass) sits at 31.6% / 1.54 ms — the recommended online default. For batch or RAG workloads where every percentage point matters, D is the answer.

\mathrm{recommend}(\text{default}) = \arg\max_{s} \frac{\mathrm{quality}(s)}{\log(\mathrm{latency}(s))}

5. Multi-Agent Concurrent Stores

20 parallel agents · zero write conflicts

Robust20 agents
Figure 5 — 5. Multi-Agent Concurrent Stores

We stress-tested MATHIR with up to 20 concurrent agents calling store() in parallel. Every scenario reached 100% success and zero write conflicts. Throughput scales ~17× from 1 to 20 agents (1.98 → 33.6 QPS). Median store latency drops from 504 ms to 38 ms under load because the LRU cache warms up.

6. Multi-Modal Fusion

Text · Image · Audio · Video → shared embedding space

Modality-agnostic4 encoders
Figure 6 — 6. Multi-Modal Fusion

MATHIR is modality-agnostic. It only sees fixed-dim vectors. BERT encodes text, CLIP encodes images, Whisper encodes audio, VideoCLIP encodes video. All four land in the same shared embedding space, which MATHIR then indexes across its 4 tiers.

7. Theoretical Foundations (6 Theorems)

Each theorem reduces to a classical result — fully proven

6 theoremsAll proven
Figure 7 — 7. Theoretical Foundations (6 Theorems)

MATHIR's correctness rests on 6 formal theorems, each proven in docs/PROOFS.md by reduction to a classical result. Theorem 1 uses Shannon's AWGN capacity; Theorem 2 uses Hoeffding concentration; Theorem 3 uses Robbins-Monro; Theorem 4 uses Neyman-Pearson; Theorem 5 uses Olshausen-Field sparse coding; Theorem 6 uses Sinkhorn-Knopp convergence.

\Pr(\mathrm{Acc}(K) \geq 1 - CKL\eta/N) \geq 1 - \exp(-N/2) \quad\text{(Theorem 2)}

8. Version Evolution (V1 → V7.8.0)

5 months · 8 releases · 62+ unit tests

V7.8.0 latestV1–V3 legacy
Figure 8 — 8. Version Evolution (V1 → V7.8.0)

From a 3-tier CNN+MLP prototype (V1, January 2026) to a doctoral-grade memory system with 8 novel algorithms, 6 formal theorems, and 4 retrieval approaches (V7.8.0, June 2026). V7.8.0 is the current release, with a 5–12× latency speedup via an LRU result cache and adaptive re-ranking.

⚡ GPU Embedding Engine (V7.8)

Default Model BAAI/bge-large-en-v1.5 (1024d)
Device NVIDIA RTX 4060 (CUDA)
Save Latency 43ms (warmed up)
Recall Latency 25ms
Architecture Persistent daemon (TCP:7338) + VecMemory (sqlite-vec)
